extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r4 (renal_impairment AND asthma AND immunosuppressed); rec_drug_b is set true only by r3 (renal_impairment). Both r3 and r4 share priority 50, and no other rule targets drug_a or drug_b, so priorities/tie-breaks change nothing. Whenever r4's antecedent is satisfied, renal_impairment is necessarily true, so r3 also fires. With renal_impairment=true, asthma=true, immunosuppressed=true (other inputs false), r3 sets rec_drug_b=true and r4 sets rec_drug_a=true, making both jointly true and violating the mutual-exclusion property.
